meta.title: Chess Practice for Complete Beginners | Chess9527
meta.description: Practice chess for free against three levels of computer opponent. No sign-up is needed, and every move is explained in plain language with rules and practical tips.

seo.rootTitle: Free Chess Practice for Complete Beginners | Chess9527
seo.rootDescription: Practice chess for free with no sign-up, learn piece movement, rules and useful playing habits as you play, and choose Simplified Chinese, English or Japanese.
seo.heading: Learn chess one move at a time
seo.intro: You do not need to memorize every rule first. Play White against a computer buddy, read each move in plain language, and review positions with unlimited undo and redo.
seo.featureOneBody: Choose Easy, Practice or Challenge mode and learn at your own pace.
seo.featureTwoBody: See the piece, starting square, destination and standard notation together.
seo.featureThreeTitle: Rules and tips are always available
seo.featureThreeBody: Review piece movement, castling, check, checkmate and useful opening habits at any time.
seo.featureFourBody: No account is required. Your nickname, games and progress stay in this browser.
seo.stepOne: Enter a nickname and choose an avatar style.
seo.stepTwo: Play the first move as White and wait for the computer reply.
seo.stepThree: Read the explanation and compare ideas with undo and redo.
seo.rulesDescription: Learn the board, all six pieces, check, checkmate, castling, promotion, en passant and chess notation in plain language.
seo.tipsDescription: Practical beginner advice about development, the center, king safety, piece values and reviewing a game.

welcome.title: Learn chess while you play
welcome.description: No sign-up needed. Choose a nickname and we’ll save your progress on this device.
welcome.required: Enter a nickname between 1 and 20 characters

game.engineFallback: The challenging chess buddy could not start, so the beginner buddy is playing instead.
game.illegal: That move is not legal. The position has not changed.
game.notYourTurn: It is the computer's turn. Please wait.
game.gameOver: The game is over. Start a new game to continue.

coach.title: AI Coach
coach.empty: After your first move, I’ll review each turn in short, clear sentences.
coach.fallback: AI explanations are unavailable, so the basic coach has taken over.
coach.thinking: The coach is reviewing this turn…

conversation.title: Conversation
conversation.empty: Make your first move and we’ll review the game here together.

guide.title: One thing to remember
guide.one: White begins at the bottom and moves upward. You are White.
guide.two: White moves first. Click or drag a piece, then choose a highlighted square.
guide.three: Pawns move forward but capture diagonally. Try a central pawn first.

help.title: Basic rules
help.orientation: The bottom-right square should be light. White moves up the board.
help.pieces: Kings move one square; queens move straight or diagonally; rooks straight; bishops diagonally; knights in an L and can jump; pawns move forward and capture diagonally.
help.check: When the king is attacked, it is in check and the next move must answer it. A king may not move into attack.
help.special: A pawn promotes on the last rank. A king and rook can castle when the conditions are met.

reset.title: Switch learner?
reset.description: This permanently deletes the nickname, progress and unfinished game saved in this browser.
